title: 10 Things Men Call 'Struggling With'
caption:
The language matters.

"Struggling with" sounds humble. It sounds like you're working on it. But for most men, it's the word they use when they've decided to keep something and need a name that doesn't sound like a choice.

Scripture has harder words. Sin. Idolatry. Cowardice. Unbelief. Not because God is harsh but because precise language is the first step toward change. You can't kill something you've given a soft name.

This isn't a condemnation. It's a reframe. The man who calls a thing what it is has already done something the man who "struggles" never gets to do — he's seen it clearly. And you can only kill what you can see.

"Put to death, therefore, whatever belongs to your earthly nature." — Colossians 3:5. Not manage. Not tame. Put to death.

Save this and send it to the brother who keeps "working on it."

#faith #christianmen #discipline #godlymen #manofgod #killsin #selfcontrol #masculinity #christianity #scripture
theme: He runs his life — his impulses don't control him.
